title: ALS emergency transport medical necessity
rule_text: For ALS emergency ambulance transport, documentation should show an emergency response, abnormal assessment findings, ALS-level monitoring or intervention, and transport to an appropriate facility.

title: Non-emergency stretcher necessity
rule_text: Non-emergency ambulance claims should document why the patient could not be transported safely by wheelchair, private vehicle, or other lower-acuity mode.

title: Signature or valid exception
rule_text: Claims must include a patient signature, authorized representative signature, or documented signature exception such as refusal, inability to sign, or crew-attested emergency condition.

title: Medicaid scheduled transport documentation
rule_text: Scheduled non-emergency transports should include destination, loaded mileage, medical necessity, and authorization reference when required by the plan.

title: Loaded mileage requirement
rule_text: Emergency ambulance claims must include loaded mileage from pickup to destination and should reconcile with the billing mileage line.

title: BLS emergency transport support
rule_text: BLS emergency transport should show acute symptoms, crew assessment, monitoring during transport, and why ambulance transport was reasonable for the patient condition.

title: Appropriate facility destination
rule_text: Documentation should identify the pickup location, destination, and clinical reason the destination was appropriate for the patient need.

title: Self-pay documentation summary
rule_text: Self-pay demo claims should include clear transport reason, loaded mileage, pickup, destination, and patient or representative signature when available.

title: Emergency dispatch and response clarity
rule_text: Emergency ambulance records should document dispatch context, patient complaint, assessment findings, and transport destination.

title: PCR completeness baseline
rule_text: A complete ambulance claim should include chief complaint or reason for transport, pickup, destination, mileage, medical necessity, and signature or exception.
